Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mintty input: replace input() with _input() using sys.stdin.readline

Python's input() on Windows uses the Windows console API (ReadConsole/
WriteConsole), which is invisible in mintty — prompts never appear and
responses are lost. _input() uses sys.stdout.write + sys.stdin.readline
for regular file I/O that works correctly through mintty's pipe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eliminating duplicated _call_qa() in ralph.py. MCP tool responses
are now self-contained with QA verdicts.

Fix no-unsafe-return for process.env in getApiBase

Locally @types/bun provides a typed ProcessEnv so process.env
indexing returns string | undefined. In CI without that type,
it resolves as any, triggering no-unsafe-return. Assign to a
typed local variable to make it safe regardless of env types.

commit.message:
fix(ui-server): restore index.html on unclean process exit [#914] (#915)

Register a process.on('exit') handler in createIndexHtmlStasher that
restores index.html when the process exits without a clean shutdown.
The 'exit' event fires for crashes, uncaught exceptions, and signal
handlers that call process.exit() — covering the gap where SIGINT/
SIGTERM/SIGHUP handlers couldn't run.

The only unrecoverable scenario is SIGKILL, which the existing crash
recovery in stash() already handles on the next startup.

Closes #914

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui-compiler): distinguish reactive reads from stable closure references [#907] (#913)

The reactivity analyzer now tracks property accesses on signal API variables
to determine whether a dependency is a reactive read (.data, .error, .loading)
or a stable reference (.refetch(), .revalidate(), .dispose()). Only reactive
reads trigger computed classification.

Previously, any const referencing a signal API var was wrapped in computed(),
even callbacks that only called plain methods. This was wasteful — the
computed re-created the function on every evaluation for no reason.

Closes #907

Co-authored-by: vertz-dev-front[bot] <2828126+vertz-dev-front[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix UnicodeEncodeError on Windows by reconfiguring stdout to UTF-8

CP1252 (default Windows console encoding) can't encode box-drawing
characters. Reconfigure stdout/stderr to UTF-8 at startup on win32.

commit.message:
perf: reduce unnecessary allocations in detection modules (#755) (#763)

* perf: reduce unnecessary allocations in detection modules (#755)

- BFS visited set: HashSet<String> → HashSet<&str> in dead_neuron.rs (2.5× faster)
- Synapse dedup: HashSet<(String,String)> → HashSet<(&str,&str)> in skip_connection.rs
- Vec::with_capacity hints added to 30+ detection function accumulators
- Dead neuron detection 29-36% faster on deep networks
- New benchmark suite benches/bfs_allocation.rs for regression tracking

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: auto-increment versions for changed projects

* Update Cargo.toml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: explain OPERATION_MODE and API key tiers across all deployment docs

Adds a new ## Operating Modes section to DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md covering:
- What OPERATION_MODE=demo vs production actually changes (demo data vs real
  data, API keys ignored in demo mode)
- How each deployment method (demo compose, prod compose, k8s) sets the mode —
  including the k8s caveat that manifests don't set it, so bare k8s deploys
  default to demo data even with the full React UI running
- API key tier table (free / freemium / paid) and per-service graceful
  degradation behaviour when keys are absent
- How to add keys via .env, k8s Secret, or the Settings UI at runtime
- Three-step summary of what you need for fully live intelligence

Also adds a callout note to README.md Demo vs. Full Platform section and
expands the USER_GUIDE.md Settings page with a full Layer 1 / Layer 2
explanation including a k8s gotcha note and per-tier degradation table.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent subpath traversal attacks in source parsing and fetching

Add SanitizeSubpath() to reject ".." segments and IsSubpathSafe() to
validate resolved paths stay within base directories. Applied at all
subpath assignment points in source parsing, URL scheme parsing, and
fetch operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix test failures and harden CI parity

- Upgrade ESLint to recommendedTypeChecked with strict rules
  (no-unnecessary-condition, no-explicit-any as error)
- Fix all resulting type errors across mobile and server
- Add root bunfig.toml so `bun test` from root picks up mobile
  mocks (preload + .mp3 loader)
- Fix livekit.test.ts: clear env vars before import so roomService
  is null regardless of dev environment
- Fix test_content.test.ts: skipIf for DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL
- Fix test_combat_tools.py: add missing update_player_hp mock to
  death save test (intermittent CI failure on nat 20)
- Harden pre-push hook: strip external API env vars from server
  and agent tests to match CI's clean environment
- Fix CI eslint step: add --max-warnings 0 to match local config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add USER_GUIDE.md — page-by-page walkthrough of all 16 UI pages

Covers every section, tab, button, and workflow for the full React frontend:
login, Dashboard, Investigations (list + Kanban + saved searches + freshness
badges), New Investigation (6 templates), Investigation Detail (7-stage
workflow, findings, entities, pivots, correlations, timeline, collaboration),
Graph Intelligence (nodes/edges, PageRank, betweenness, community detection,
blast radius), Threat Analysis (MITRE ATT&CK matrix + Navigator export),
Analytic Workbench (Admiralty scale, ACH matrix, IC confidence, evidence
chain), Reports (PDF + STIX 2.1), Threat Intelligence (26-actor dossiers +
TTP overlap scoring), Compliance (GDPR/CCPA/HIPAA), Credential Intelligence
(HIBP/Dehashed/Hudson Rock/paste + k-anonymity password check), Monitoring
(watchlists + snapshot diffing), Team (roles + audit trail), Data Sources
(MCP server status), and Settings (API key management + mode toggle).
Cross-cutting features section covers Command Palette, live collaboration,
unified IOC enrichment, entity hover cards, freshness badges, and evidence
chains.

Also adds USER_GUIDE.md to the Documentation table in README.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add .. escape to return to main menu from any prompt

Any ask/ask_bool/ask_choice prompt now accepts '..' to raise GoBack
and return to the main menu. Header shows the hint on every tool screen.

commit.message:
Fix circular discussions and model selection mismatch

  build on prior points, and progress the conversation forward.
  Includes turn counter for context.
- Fix model name resolution: default config values like "mistral"
  now auto-resolve to actual Ollama names like "mistral:latest"
  when models load, preventing the select dropdown from silently
  falling back to the wrong model.

docs: add UI navigation guide to Demo vs. Full Platform section

Explains the three ways to reach all 16 pages in the full React frontend:
- Left sidebar (Main + Administration sections, with Investigations expanding
  to Active / History / Saved sub-items; collapses to icon-only mode)
- Investigation-scoped tabs (Graph, Threats, Workbench reached from within
  an open investigation)
- New Investigation button on the list page
- Command Palette (⌘K / Ctrl+K) for keyboard-first navigation from anywhere
